fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Michigan CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Michigan - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity michigan, how to transfer CNA license to michigan, michigan CNA endorsement application

board.name: Michigan LARA
board.mailing_address:
Bureau of Professional Licensing
P.O. Box 30670
Lansing, MI 48909

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Michigan.

steps.title: Complete Approved Training Program
steps.description: Enroll in and successfully complete a Nurse Aide Training Program approved by the Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA). You must receive at least 75 hours of training, including 16 hours of supervised practical training.

steps.title: Register for and Pass the Competency Exam
steps.description: Once training is complete, register for and successfully pass both parts (written/oral and skills) of the Michigan Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Examination administered by Prometric. Visit Prometric.com/nurseaide/mi for exam registration and information.

steps.title: Placement on the Nurse Aide Registry
steps.description: Upon successful completion of the Prometric examination, Prometric will report your results to LARA. Your name will then be added to the Michigan Nurse Aide Registry, certifying you as a CNA in Michigan. No separate application is generally required for initial certification by examination.

steps.title: Apply for Endorsement (Out-of-State CNAs)
steps.description: If you are a CNA certified in another state, download and complete the 'Nurse Aide Registry Application' (LARA-NURS-APP-001) from the Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA) Nurse Aide Registry page. Submit the completed application with all required documentation, including proof of current certification in good standing from your previous state, to LARA via mail.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Michigan CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $20. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Michigan.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Michigan?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Michigan while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in MI.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Michigan.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Michigan
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) in Michigan, you must first complete an approved training program and pass the Michigan Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Examination administered by Prometric. Out-of-state CNAs applying by endorsement must submit a paper application directly to the Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA).

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Michigan
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Michigan CNA certification.

official_links.portal_name: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA) Nurse Aide Registry Page
official_links.registration_steps: For initial certification by examination, there is no direct online application portal with LARA; the process involves completing approved training and passing the Prometric exam. For endorsement (out-of-state CNAs), a paper application form must be downloaded, completed, and mailed to LARA.
